political.s: FEC: $26K PAC donations; 59% to Republican, 41% to Democratic committees.

charity.s: $25M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.09% of revenue) via Kraft Heinz Foundation. Operates the Kraft Heinz Company Foundation: $2.9M in charitable contributions, gifts & grants paid (FY2023, IRS Form 990-PF).
charity.amt: $12 million (2021 commitment over 3 years); multiple ongoing donations
charity.focus: Global hunger elimination, malnutrition, food security, children's nutrition

environment.s: environmental violation: $10.9M in federal penalties (Violation Tracker).

labor.s: 114 federal penalties totaling $124.1M since 2000 (Violation Tracker). Supply-chain signals: KnowTheChain 35/100; CHRB 9.8/26; TVPRA exposure; AU MS statement.

dei.s: HRC Corporate Equality Index: 100/100 — Equality 100 Leader (2025). Top-rated LGBTQ+ workplace policies, benefits, and culture. HRC Corporate Equality Index 100/100 (2025) — top-tier LGBTQ+ workplace equality rating.

animals.s: BBFAW 2024 Tier 4 — policy established but limited audited progress disclosure. [BBFAW Tier 4]

execPay.s: CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio 170:1 — SEC proxy statement (DEF 14A, 2026).

wiki.controversies:
==Controversies==
In 2017, the claim that the Kraft Parmesan Cheese label was misleading was rejected by judges. The label reads "Kraft 100% Grated Parmesan Cheese". It was argued that a "reasonable consumer" would know the product could not be 100% cheese, as any dairy product degrades quickly without preservatives.

On December 1, 2023, a federal jury in Illinois awarded $17.8 million to several large food companies, including Kraft Heinz, in a lawsuit against Cal-Maine Foods, Inc. and others over allegations of egg price fixing. Cal-Maine Foods, contesting the verdict, insists on its innocence and the absence of wrongdoing in its business practices.
wiki.extract: The Kraft Heinz Company (KHC), commonly known as Kraft Heinz, is an American multinational food company formed by the merger of Kraft Foods Group, Inc. and the H.J. Heinz Company co-headquartered in Chicago and Pittsburgh.

cases.caseName: Cooper v. Kraft Heinz Foods Company
Kraft Heinz Company v. The United Food and Commercial Workers International Union AFL-CIO, Local Union 431

sampleRecords.event_description: Caught, entangled in running powered equipment  normal operation
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was using a pneumatic tool to clamp drawers together. The material broke and the employee's left hand was caught in the drawer panel. The employee's middle fingertip was amputated.

sampleRecords.event_description: Caught, entangled in running powered equipment  normal operation
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was using a machine to cut and split wood for door frames when his left index finger came in contact with the running blade, amputating the finger.

sampleRecords.nature: Effects of heat and light, n.e.c.
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee working in a paint shop became overheated and dehydrated due to heat. He was hospitalized.

sampleRecords.event_description: Caught in running equipment or machinery during maintenance, cleaning
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was replacing a dust cover on an oven fan. The fan blade caught the edge of the employee's right hand, lacerating the top of the hand and the little finger, breaking the ring finger, and cutting a tendon.

sampleRecords.event_description: Struck by swinging part of powered vehicle
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was working on a rollercoaster. The employee was struck by a 300- to 400-pound wooden bolster (which was being lifted by a forklift) and suffered a broken right tibia and a broken nose.

news_items.title: Kraft Teases TSA‑Friendly Ranch Dressing Packets After World Cup Fans Obsess Over Condiment - People.com
news_items.summary: People.com reported that Kraft teased a TSA-friendly ranch dressing packet product in response to World Cup tourist interest in the condiment.

news_items.title: Kraft teases new 'TSA-compliant' ranch amid World Cup tourist hype - 10TV
news_items.summary: 10TV reported that Kraft is teasing a new TSA-compliant ranch dressing product amid heightened consumer interest during the World Cup.

news_items.title: Kraft works on TSA-compliant ranch after World Cup fans pack bottles - Scripps News
news_items.summary: Kraft announced it is working on a TSA-compliant ranch dressing packet following viral attention from World Cup fans bringing ranch bottles to matches.

recent_events.summary: Kraft teased the development of TSA-friendly single-serve ranch dressing packets following viral attention from World Cup fans carrying full bottles through airport security.
Kraft announced it is working on TSA-compliant ranch dressing packaging in response to World Cup tourists traveling with full-size condiment bottles.
